entities.name: ENZYME-LINKED IMMUNOSORBENT ASSAY (ELISA)
entities.description: A biochemical technique used for the enumeration of rhizobia, noted for being faster than plant infection techniques but requiring a minimum of 10^2-10^3 cells for detection.

entities.name: RHIZOBIA
entities.description: Soil bacteria that are the subject of enumeration using ELISA and plant infection techniques.

entities.name: PEAT CARRIER
entities.description: A medium used for carrying rhizobia in which their population is enumerated.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The natural medium in which rhizobia are found and enumerated.

entities.name: PLANT INFECTION TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A conventional method for enumerating rhizobia, which is slower than ELISA and used when other micro-organisms are present.

entities.name: MICRO-ORGANISMS
entities.description: Other biological entities present in the environment alongside rhizobia.
